other_observers: Barbara Byron, Bob Thompson, Sue Agrinoff, and Judy Lane. None had good looks at birds.; This year Jim Beatty to my knowledge.
specific_location: Yellowjacket Creek about 20 miles west of US 160 off of CR G, McElmo Creek Road. About two miles north of CR G on unmarked dirt road.
description: Bird was small verivora warbler with very drab gray coloring. Bill was dark, thin and short to medium in length for size of bird. head and upperparts were medium gray. Dark eye was surrounded by indistinct, off-white eyering on otherwise plain face. There were no wing bars. Underparts were lighter and somewhat buffy. Undertail coverts were white. Tail was short and dark. Overall appearance of bird was similar to a kinglet, but no wing flicking. Bird stayed fairly high in cottonwoods and was very active. As a result I did not observe the rump or crown. I also saw the female which was quite similar, but slightly less colorful.Behaviors: Birds were very actively feeding and flying around an area about 300 to 500 feet along the creek. They generally stayed at the mid-height or higher in the cottonwoods with only short observation periods. I examined the 2004 nest site, but birds were apparently not using it this year. They seemed to be using an area slightly farther downstream.Call: Both heard. Song was fairly loud, sweet, rapid, and fairly long, but pitch did not change much. Flight call was a soft tset.Plumage: Alternate
